title: Respond at personal-ledger scale
description: Measure interactive External Orchestrator latency at personal-ledger scale during initial CLI field validation without making a fixed latency threshold a module-completion condition.
target: For the initial CLI field-validation release, actuals, reconciliation-candidate, completed-scope, and ordinary-write benchmarks report p50 and p95 latency; no fixed latency value blocks module completion, while exact membership, totals, grouping, pagination, reconciliation, and mutation outcomes remain mandatory.
load_condition: Three accounts, 100000 active transactions, two Spend Pool, representative category, payment-attribution, evidence, reconciliation, correction, and relationship history, one OS identity, and one writer; record the host and concurrent workload with every result.
measurement: Run thirty warmed repetitions per representative actuals, reconciliation-candidate, coverage, and ordinary-write scenario using monotonic elapsed time; report p50, p95, environment, and correctness. Latency is advisory until real-world CLI evidence establishes a new blocking target.
rationale: Dirk prioritizes starting real-world CLI validation over pre-release latency tuning. Retaining repeatable measurements exposes regressions and supplies evidence for a later threshold without delaying usability feedback.
